landing.subtitle: Savings groups with real commitment. No fees, no middlemen — just you, your friends, and a smart vault.
landing.trustLine: Vault protected by an audited contract. No admin key, no custody.
landing.previewNudgeBody: Ana finished her deposit. Your turn by Friday.
landing.momentsTitle: Deposit, get a heads-up, hit the goal.
landing.moment1Body: One tap. Your wallet signs. The vault records.
landing.moment2Body: If someone's late, the group gets a gentle reminder — no one has to argue.
landing.moment3Body: End of cycle, the contract releases to those who showed up. Automatic.
landing.whyTitleTail: It's the oldest way of saving there is.
landing.whyIntro: Consórcio, caixinha, vaquinha, junta, tanda. Academics call it ROSCA — a group that agrees on an amount, a schedule, and trusts each other to close the cycle. SafeNudge is the same idea — with the tedious parts (chasing, reminding, holding) solved in a contract.
landing.stat1Label: more savings with a commitment device
landing.stat2Label: higher balance in peer-savings accounts vs. solo accounts
landing.stat2Cite: Kast, Meier & Pomeranz · Review of Econ & Stat · 2018
landing.stat3Label: Brazilians in active consórcios — the country's oldest rotating-group savings model
landing.stat4Label: more than control: fixed-installment commitment saved $10.20 vs $0.64 in 130 days
landing.stat4Cite: John · When Commitment Fails · Field experiment · 2018
landing.stat5Label: more effective having an external referee than promising yourself
landing.stat5Cite: Ariely · Harvard · self-imposed vs. external deadlines
landing.stat6Label: success rate when money is on the line and someone verifies — the group is that referee
landing.quoteText: You can't save alone. People join a group
landing.quoteCite: — ROSCA participants quoted in Gugerty · U. Washington · 2007
landing.quoteExplainer: What makes it work is what the literature calls a commitment device: the deposit is public, and you can't pull it back. Showing up or not stops being a private decision — it becomes a conversation with people you respect.

comoFunciona.sub: Every SafeNudge group moves through the same three tempos: first you agree, then you deliver together, then you receive. The vault only releases money when each part happens at the rhythm you chose.

pillars.name: Agree
pillars.lede: Before the first cent, the group decides everything — together, in plain words. After that, the contract remembers.

points.title: Who's in
points.body: Only people you invite. Groups are private — no one discovers them, no one asks to join. Max 20, min 3.

points.title: How much, when
points.body: Monthly amount, deposit day, cycle length. The group picks — R$100 or R$5,000, 3 months or 24.

points.title: Late penalty rate
points.body: The group sets a percentage (e.g. 5%) deducted from anyone who's late. That deduction becomes a bonus for members who followed through — and that's the only place SafeNudge earns (small fraction).

points.title: It all becomes contract
points.body: The rules become Anchor code on Solana. No one — not us, not the creator — can change them later without a vote.

pillars.name: Deliver
pillars.lede: Month by month, each person deposits. The money sits in the vault until cycle end. When someone forgets, the group reminds — no drama, no collection calls.

points.title: Nudge before
points.body: The app nudges 3 days before, 1 day before, morning of. Gentle. No red letters, no scare tactics.

points.title: Group heads-up
points.body: If the day passes, any member can send a heads-up. A notification arrives — no amount, no demand, just a human reminder.

points.title: Automatic penalty
points.body: 48h after the delay, the contract deducts the percentage the group agreed to. That amount goes into the bonus for members who followed through — and SafeNudge keeps a small fraction.

points.title: All visible
points.body: Who's deposited, who's missing, how much is saved. No ranking, no shame — just transparency so no one gets lost.

pillars.name: Receive
pillars.lede: In the final month, the vault releases to everyone at once. No order, no draw, no "who goes first" — you started together, you finish together.

points.title: Everyone in the same moment
points.body: It's not a rotating caixinha. Each person receives the total they deposited plus their share of the group's accumulated penalty bonus.

points.title: Instant
points.body: The transaction happens in seconds on Solana. No business days, no clearing, no manual release — just the contract executing.

points.title: No fees for people who follow through
points.body: You get back 100% of what you deposited. The only deductions are Solana network fees (fractions of a cent) — and only latecomers pay the penalty.

points.title: And it cycles again
points.body: At cycle end, the group decides: run again with the same crew, change the amount, or wrap. It's a caixinha, not a prison.

comoFunciona.defaultsSub: We designed the worst case first. Here's what the contract does — automatically — when a deposit doesn't land.

defaults.body: Only the missing person sees it. No one else is notified. Most late deposits are just forgetfulness.
Any member can send a heads-up. Gentle notification, no amount, no demand.
Agreed percentage (e.g. 5%) is deducted from the next deposit. Becomes a bonus for members who followed through. SafeNudge keeps a small fraction.
The group votes on replacing. Penalty bonus already deducted accrues to those who stayed.

comoFunciona.defaultsFoot: In 94% of cycles, only Day 0 happens. The rest exists for when you need it.

faq.q: Do I have to trust everyone in the group?
faq.a: You need to trust the vault. The escrow and automatic rules cover the moments when human trust fails — because it does sometimes.

faq.q: Who holds the money while it waits?
faq.a: The contract. Not SafeNudge, not the group creator, not a partner bank. The balance is visible on-chain the whole time.

Zero if you follow through. The only costs are Solana network fees — fractions of a cent per transaction, just to keep the network running. SafeNudge only earns when someone's late: we take a small slice of the penalty that the group itself set. If no one's late, we don't charge anything.

You can, but your deposits already in the vault stay there until cycle end — everyone receives together at the end. If you leave without completing, the agreed penalty kicks in and becomes a bonus for the rest of the group.

faq.q: What if SafeNudge goes out of business?
faq.a: The vault keeps working. It's an open contract on the blockchain — you can recover everything directly, even without the app. We make things easier, but we don't lock you in.

faq.q: Does the money earn yield while it waits?
faq.a: If the group wants, yes — there's a stablecoin yield option with treasury-rate returns. Opt-in, with a visible button to turn it off.

comoFunciona.ctaSub: Create a group with people who already trust you. Or join one with an open seat.
